content.method: Difference-in-differences or event-study design using settlement-level panel data on school closures, population counts, births, and out-migration 2001-2025, matched with propensity-score or synthetic control comparisons against similar non-closure villages; replicate Finnish/Danish quasi-experimental designs with Hungarian registry data.
content.question: What is the causal effect of village school closure on subsequent out-migration and depopulation in Hungary specifically, net of pre-existing socioeconomic and demographic trends?
content.why_it_matters: Nearly every proposal (default-closure, presumption-against-closure, grade-split, networked retention) implicitly assumes an answer to whether closure accelerates decline or merely follows it. Without this, mitigation packages and safeguard designs cannot be calibrated, and the entire policy debate rests on contested international analogies (Finland, Denmark, Poland) that may not transfer.
